title: trim bd prime output: cap bd memories auto-injected drawer narratives
description:
**From loom-nsb research (drawer_loom_decisions_3eec30046461f0766ac92eec).**

`bd prime` output is ~147 KB per fire in liza_base. Most of that is verbose drawer narratives auto-injected via `bd memories` keyword matches. Sample (5.4 MB liza_base transcript): bd prime fires 6× per session = ~900 KB before deduplication; even after the duplicate-registration fix is applied, each unique fire is still 147 KB.

## The cost

bd prime content sits in the SessionStart prefix and re-bills every turn until /clear. The bd workflow primer (~5 KB) is fine; the verbose memory drawer dumps are where the bloat is.

## Two paths

### A. Loom-side wrapper hook (loom scope)

Replace the direct `bd prime` SessionStart registration with a loom wrapper that:
- Calls bd prime
- Filters / caps the bd memories section (e.g. truncate each memory to 200 chars; cap total memories block at ~10 KB)
- Emits the trimmed output

Pro: doesn't depend on upstream beads cooperation. Con: brittle if bd prime output format changes.

### B. Upstream beads issue (out of loom scope)

File against the beads project: bd prime should support flags like `--memories-cap=10K` or `--no-memories-narrative` (titles only). loom installs would adopt the trimmed mode by default.

Pro: clean separation of concerns. Con: depends on beads team timeline.

## Recommendation

Start with A (loom wrapper) for immediate relief, then file B upstream and migrate to it when shipped. Document the wrapper as a temporary measure in its source.

## Estimated savings

5-10% of session bytes (post-duplicate-fix). Compounds with the duplicate-bd-prime fix.

## Acceptance

- Loom ships a bd-prime-wrapper.sh hook that produces a trimmed output.
- Test fixture: input = full bd prime output → wrapper output is ≤30 KB.
- Documented in docs/reference/.
- (Optionally) upstream issue filed against beads with link.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-09 via loom-nsb research. Compounds with the duplicate-bd-prime liza_base fix bead — that one fixes WHERE bd prime fires, this one fixes WHAT bd prime emits.

title: audit-project: detect duplicate plugin-registered hook commands across project + plugin settings
description:
**Preventive bead from loom-nsb research.**

The duplicate `bd prime` SessionStart + PreCompact registration in liza_base (sibling bead) is a config-level drift that loom's `/audit-project` should detect, so future projects don't accumulate the same waste.

## Detection logic

For each command registered in a project's `.claude/settings.json` hooks block:
- Cross-reference against commands registered by enabled plugins' `plugin.json` hooks blocks.
- If the same command (e.g. `bd prime`) is registered for the same hook event (e.g. `SessionStart`) at both layers, emit a [HOOK DUP] warning naming both registration sites and the recommended fix (drop the project-level dup).

## Scope

- Extend existing `/audit-project` SKILL.md with new check (call it Check 5 or insert into hook-related checks).
- Read project's `.claude/settings.json` hooks tree.
- Enumerate enabled plugins via `~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/*/claude-plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (or the plugin-resolution path Claude Code actually uses).
- Compare command + matcher + hook-event tuples; flag exact duplicates.

## Acceptance

- Check fires on liza_base (today's known case) and surfaces the bd-prime dup.
- Check is silent on a clean project (no duplicates).
- Test fixture: synthetic project with a duplicated hook → check warns.
- Documented in audit-project's SKILL.md with the dup-pattern explanation.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-09 via loom-nsb research. Preventive: catches the duplicate-bd-prime pattern in future projects. Pairs with the liza_base-specific fix bead.

Add support for env var `LOOM_SUBAGENT_LEAN=1` which forces the loom SessionStart hooks into slim mode regardless of detected subagent context. Lets app code wrap subprocess Claude Code invocations with the env var set.

Low-priority escape hatch; the main fix is heuristic-driven detection.

title: docs-scaffold next-steps line 269 names wrong GH Pages source for mkdocs gh-deploy workflow
description:
The /docs-scaffold 'Next steps' summary at skills/docs-scaffold/SKILL.md:269 tells the user to set GitHub Pages 'Source = GitHub Actions'. But the scaffolded workflow at templates/diataxis/.github/workflows/docs.yml uses 'mkdocs gh-deploy --force --no-history', which pushes the built site to a gh-pages branch. The correct repo setting for that deploy path is 'Source = Deploy from a branch -> gh-pages', NOT 'GitHub Actions'. As written, a user following the next-steps text verbatim will end up with a non-publishing site.

Two fix options:
A. Update line 269 text to 'Source = Deploy from a branch -> gh-pages (one-time setup)'.
B. Switch the workflow to use actions/deploy-pages (which IS the 'Source = GitHub Actions' path) and keep line 269 as-is.

Option A is the smaller change. Option B aligns with GitHub's currently-recommended Pages deploy mechanism (Pages site as deployment target, no gh-pages branch sprawl). Pick one before fixing.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-09 during a question about whether loom auto-publishes scaffolded docs. The automation IS in place — only the next-steps text is wrong.

title: dispatch operational: API 529 overload kills mid-flight + resume agents; resilience playbook needed
description:
When the Anthropic API is overloaded (529), parallel-dispatched agents hit the failure mode at three different stages:

1. **Mid-flight crash** — agent has done substantial work, hits a 529 on a Sonnet call, dies. Worktree filesystem state preserved; bd state may be in transit.
2. **Resume crash** — orchestrator dispatches a resume agent, which dies in 4 seconds with 0 tool uses because the API is too sick to even start. Resume can't begin until API health recovers.
3. **Sustained outage** — multiple agents in a wave can crash within seconds of each other. The orchestrator detects 3 crashes and stops dispatching to avoid burning context.

## Concrete case (liza_base 2026-05-06)

- All 3 Wave C agents (wx7, 982, 9uo) crashed on a 529 burst.
- The 9uo resume agent died in 4s with 0 tool uses on the next dispatch attempt.
- wx7 actually came back successfully on a subsequent independent retry.

## Implications

The harness routes ALL agent calls through the same Anthropic API backend. There is no 'go around' path; subprocess `claude` CLI from agents hits the same backend. When overload sustains, the entire parallel-dispatch flow is wedged.

## Proposed playbook (loom-level)

1. **Detect API overload** — when ≥2 agents in a wave crash with 529 within a short window, surface a 'API health pause' state to the orchestrator. Don't keep dispatching agents that immediately die.
2. **Preserve WIP** — when an agent crashes, capture its worktree filesystem state into a recovery surface (loom-managed) so resume agents can rebuild without depending on rebase choreography preserving untracked files.
3. **Resume-from-WIP recipe** — formalize the 'check what previous agent left, verify it, finish what's left' shape into a recipe that resume briefs can reference. Today this is ad-hoc per case.
4. **Decision-drawer-as-recovery-surface** — promote 'file decision drawer FIRST' from convention to recipe-mandatory. The drawer's body should be detailed enough to rebuild the implementation; it's the only crash-resilient artifact.

## Sibling concern

Once API health returns, mid-flight resume timing is delicate. Resume too early: dies again. Resume too late: untracked WIP grows stale relative to main. loom could add an exponential-backoff retry loop OR explicit 'health probe before resume' step.

## Provenance

Surfaced 2026-05-06 during liza_base FRH Wave C dispatch — API 529 burst killed 3 simultaneous agents.

title: audit-project Check 2a script: 'every bd-ID shows as dead' is a regex bug, not real drift
description:
During loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-05 verification phase, the parallel agent ran '/audit-project --check=docs' as the e63 acceptance check. Check 2a (bd-ID resolution — does each bead-ID cited in docs/ resolve via 'bd show'?) initially reported every ID as 'dead'. The agent flagged this directly: 'every ID showing as "dead" looks suspicious' and re-implemented with simpler logic. Result: 0/49 dead — the original was a script bug.

The agent's commentary on the fix suggests the original script used some kind of complex regex or output-parsing that broke on the liza_base bd-prefix shape ('liza_base-XXX' instead of just 'XXX' or a project-less form). The fix used 'simpler logic' that worked.

Required investigation:
1. Find the audit-project Check 2a implementation.
2. Determine what was breaking on liza_base's bd-IDs but worked on tla-puzzles' (where the trial didn't surface this bug).
3. Replace with the agent's working approach (or write a fresh implementation that handles all bd-ID formats).
4. Add a test fixture covering at least: project-prefixed IDs ('liza_base-e63', 'tla-puzzles-bwv'), bare IDs ('77oy'), and mixed.

Severity: P3 — false-positive 'every ID dead' result is alarming but the agent caught it by sanity check ('looks suspicious'). A non-vigilant agent might have proceeded to file noise as drift. Worth fixing.

Source: drawer drawer_liza_base_decisions_cbab33a93655a34b50ab0390 (liza_base/decisions, audit summary section). Trial: loom-wxo.

title: bd post-commit hook re-export loop: each commit triggers another export-needing commit
description:
Extension of loom-cka. After 'bd hooks install' and the absorbing-commit pattern (which loom-cka added to project-onboarder docs), there's a deeper pattern: every git commit triggers bd's pre-commit hook to re-export .beads/issues.jsonl, which then needs ANOTHER commit to absorb. The loop:

1. User commits something logical (Commit A).
2. bd's pre-commit hook re-exports .beads/issues.jsonl (post-staging diff appears).
3. User commits the export reconcile (Commit B = 'chore(beads): post-X bd export reconcile').
4. bd's pre-commit hook re-exports AGAIN (post-staging diff appears).
5. User commits another reconcile (Commit C = 'chore(beads): post-Y bd export reconcile').
6. ... repeats until user gives up

In the loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-05, the agent went 3 layers deep before stopping with the comment: 'The bd post-commit hook re-exports issues.jsonl after every commit — chasing the loop with another commit just retriggers it. Stopping here; the drift is pure metadata reordering and the next bd-related commit will fold it in naturally.'

That's the right call from the agent's POV but it leaves uncommitted state that future agents will pick up + commit, rinse, repeat across sessions.

Possible fixes:
(a) bd pre-commit hook should detect 'is this commit a pure issues.jsonl export with no semantic delta from the dolt store?' and skip the re-export when so. Stops the loop at its source.
(b) bd post-commit hook offers a 'reconcile_mode=batch' option that defers re-export to N seconds after the LAST commit in a batch (heuristic: 'session is still active'). User-side but eliminates the rapid-fire loop.
(c) Wrapping all bd-related commits in a single 'bd flush' command that does the export ONCE at the end of a logical batch. Manual but explicit.

Severity: P3 — annoyance, not correctness. The drift is pure metadata reordering; the bd database is consistent. But it makes git history noisy with 3-5 'chore(beads): post-X bd export reconcile' commits per logical session-close.

Source: drawer drawer_liza_base_decisions_cbab33a93655a34b50ab0390 (liza_base/decisions, post-close section). Trial: loom-wxo. Pattern observed in liza_base/main commit chain: 4879035 → 8123836 → aed8096.

Extends: loom-cka (which fixed the FIRST commit case via absorbing-commit guidance; this bead is the LOOPING case).

title: bd-close hook can't see just-filed drawer/KG/diary artifacts; capture-then-close fails timing race
description:
The bd-close-capture hook (PreToolUse on 'bd close') verifies that drawer + KG + diary artifacts were captured before allowing the close. In the loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-05, the parallel session:

1. Dispatched drawer-author + kg-relationship-extractor agents in parallel
2. Both returned solid artifacts (drawer + 10 KG triples + diary delta)
3. Filed all artifacts via mempalace_add_drawer / mempalace_kg_add / mempalace_diary_write
4. Ran 'bd close liza_base-e63'
5. Hook BLOCKED — couldn't see the artifacts despite them being filed seconds earlier
6. Agent bypassed with BD_CLOSE_FORCE=1

The race: the hook's freshness check probably queries MemPalace's index/cache, which hasn't refreshed in the few-second window between filing and closing.

Possible fixes:
(a) Hook explicitly waits for index refresh before checking (e.g., poll for up to 5s, or call a 'flush index' MCP method first).
(b) Hook accepts artifacts filed in the current session (use a more direct mechanism than MemPalace's search index — e.g., look at the drawer files on disk under <palace>/<wing>/<room>/).
(c) Hook accepts BD_CLOSE_FORCE=1 silently (current escape hatch) but emits a warning telling the user to retry without the override after 30s. Doesn't fix the race; just makes the override less feel-bad.

Severity: P3 — the BD_CLOSE_FORCE escape hatch works, but every well-behaved capture-then-close session hits this race. Two trials, two manual workarounds. The hook is doing the right thing (verifying capture) but its check is timing-fragile.

Source: drawer drawer_liza_base_decisions_cbab33a93655a34b50ab0390 (liza_base/decisions). Trial: loom-wxo.
close_reason: Superseded by loom-8vb. The race-condition diagnosis (MemPalace index/cache lag) was wrong: the old hook performed no MemPalace query at all, so there was no race — just an unconditional block. loom-8vb (drawer drawer_loom_decisions_2fbf2d5f4c0f5e50ab84e628; commits ab1a9de, f71b631) ships real 5-matcher artifact verification via direct SQLite reads (race-free by construction), which resolves the same trial symptom. See drawer_loom_decisions_791d132361dd2875f5555e71 for the close-out.

title: audit-project: extend AUTOFIX:gitignore-worktrees to also append .claude/workflow-state.json
description:
The Wave 2 AUTOFIX:gitignore-worktrees recipe (loom-a29) only appends .claude/worktrees/ to .gitignore. But the loom session itself creates .claude/workflow-state.json at every session start (per-session ephemeral state); without a gitignore entry, it shows up as untracked at every audit and gets accidentally committed.

Observed pattern across BOTH customer trials:
- tla-puzzles (loom-b6o): had to add .claude/workflow-state.json to .gitignore as a separate commit (8f6df65 'chore: gitignore .claude/workflow-state.json (per-session ephemeral)')
- liza_base (loom-wxo): same need; same separate commit recommended

Both trials caught this manually. The AUTOFIX recipe should handle it.

Required fix: extend AUTOFIX:gitignore-worktrees to append BOTH:
- .claude/worktrees/   (existing)
- .claude/workflow-state.json   (new)

Both should be idempotent appends (skip if line already present). Consider renaming the recipe to AUTOFIX:gitignore-loom-ephemera to reflect the broader scope.

Severity: P3 ergonomics. Not a correctness bug — humans easily catch+add the missing line — but the AUTOFIX recipe exists specifically to eliminate this manual step.

Source: loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-04 + loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. Both trials encountered this same gap. Drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_1101f772a4ac7660a0f45aa1 (Wave 2 close-out).

title: audit-project: agent drafted+applied .claude/rules/tests.md despite Wave 2 explicitly excluding rules content from AUTOFIX
description:
Wave 2 (loom-a29) close-out drawer (drawer_loom_decisions_1101f772a4ac7660a0f45aa1) explicitly listed '.claude/rules/ content (content choice)' as EXCLUDED from AUTOFIX. The intent: humans should review/author rule content because it encodes project conventions.

In the loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-04, the audit treated the missing .claude/rules/tests.md as a fixable item, drafted content from the project's canonical 'V0 TESTING DISCIPLINE' MemPalace drawer + phenomenology-audit lock, and wrote the file — all without explicit human authorship/review.

Two readings:
(a) Nice initiative — the agent has full context to draft well-informed rule content from the canonical source, much better than a generic template. The existing exclusion was overly conservative.
(b) Scope creep — the rule encodes project conventions; humans should at minimum review (preferably author) the text. AUTOFIX should not silently land it.

Decision needed:
1. (a) → expand AUTOFIX scope to include 'draft .claude/rules/<file>.md from canonical MemPalace source if pointed at one'. Add a recipe-id like AUTOFIX:rules-from-drawer with an explicit drawer parameter. Document.
2. (b) → tighten the audit-project skill text to forbid drafting rules content as part of AUTOFIX; make it always emit as a MISS that requires human action.

Recommendation: probably (b) for safety, with a future-bead for (a) once we have a clear pattern of which drawer-types map cleanly to which rule files.

Severity: P3 — depends on the loom-wxo AUTOFIX-bypass investigation (loom-tbd). If the per-item gate had worked, the user would have approved the draft, and this scope-creep concern would be moot. So this bead may be a no-op once the gate is fixed.

Source: loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-04.

title: loom session-close: 'bd dolt push' no-remote should not read as failure
description:
Loom's session-close protocol (in loom CLAUDE.md and the bead-lifecycle-shell skill) runs 'bd dolt push' as part of finalizing work. In tla-puzzles (and presumably any solo bd workspace), 'bd dolt push' exits 1 with the message:

  Error: push to origin/main: Error 1105: fatal: remote 'origin' not found.
  Please make sure the remote exists.
  No remote is configured for this database.
  For solo use, pushing is optional — your issues are stored locally
  in .beads/ and versioned by Dolt automatically.

The error message itself confirms this is benign for solo workspaces. But the loom protocol surfaces it as a failure to fix. Observed in loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial 2026-05-04.

Possible fixes (pick one):
(a) Loom CLAUDE.md / bead-lifecycle-shell: run 'bd dolt remote' first; if no remote configured, skip the push step with a one-line note ('solo bd workspace; skipping bd dolt push').
(b) Wrap in shell: 'bd dolt push 2>&1 | grep -v "remote .origin. not found" || true'.
(c) Add a 'bd dolt push --solo-ok' (or '--ignore-no-remote') flag and use it in loom guidance.

(a) is the most explicit; (b) is brittle (depends on error text); (c) is a bd change, not loom.

title: bd pre-commit hook: first commit after install merges issue-export churn into unrelated changes
description:
Reproduction: after running 'bd hooks install' in a project that has uncommitted bd issue-state changes, the very next 'git commit' (regardless of subject) auto-stages '.beads/issues.jsonl' (re-exported by bd's pre-commit hook) into the commit alongside whatever the user actually intended.

Observed in loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial 2026-05-04: the user split fixes into 3 logical commits (cardinality / loom-adoption / gitignore-cleanup). The first commit (cardinality, 891d2ae) silently picked up a +6-line .beads/issues.jsonl re-export that the user did not intend in that scope. Two-things-in-one-commit against intent.

Possible fixes (pick one):
(a) 'bd hooks install' should run a pre-staging sync before completing — clear the export-pending queue so the first user commit is clean.
(b) Loom workflow guidance / project-onboarder checklist warns: 'after bd hooks install, run an empty bd-only commit (or sync) before any logical commit, to absorb the auto-export'.
(c) bd's pre-commit hook should detect 'is the user actively staging non-bd files?' and defer the auto-export to a separate commit when so.

(a) is the cleanest from the user's POV. (b) is a doc-only fix. (c) is a behavior change to bd itself, not loom.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_dfb5ece53785610c83ca6619 (loom/decisions, friction signal E). Trial: loom-b6o.

Severity: P3 ergonomics — doesn't break anything, but produces commits with mixed scope that are hard to revert cleanly.

title: audit-project: implement --apply-onboarding flag for one-line MISS items
description:
The /audit-project skill produces MISS items that are one-line bash to fix (bd hooks install; write {"v":1,"mode":"full"} to .claude/workflow.json; append .claude/worktrees/ to .gitignore). Today the user must approve+execute each one in a follow-up message.

Implement --apply-onboarding as a flag on /audit-project that:
- only applies MISS items with deterministic one-command fixes (bd hooks install; create workflow.json with default mode=full; append known entries to .gitignore)
- prompts (or skips with --yes) for any MISS item that has a real choice (e.g. workflow.json mode=light vs full — surface both as options and let user pick)
- never touches WARN items (those imply real conflict — dirty tree, etc.)
- prints what was applied for review
- leaves git in dirty state

Analogous in spirit to the --apply-trivial flag (separate bead). Pair them so a clean fresh-project audit can land most onboarding + cardinality fixes in one approved sweep, leaving only the genuinely-judgmental items for human discussion.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_598a609fc3e29b3c5937965f (loom/decisions, friction signal #4).
Trial: loom-b6o (3 MISS items: bd hooks install + workflow.json + .gitignore append).

Determinism heuristics: TRIVIAL for cardinality count drifts + unambiguous dead-bead-ID supersessions; AUTOFIX for bd-hooks (with loom-cka absorbing commit), workflow-json (default mode=full, --workflow-mode override), gitignore-worktrees (idempotent append). title: audit-project: implement --apply-trivial flag for deterministic doc fixes
description:
The /audit-project skill's report literally suggests '--apply-trivial' as a way to auto-apply the deterministic doc fixes it found ('With --apply-trivial I can apply the count corrections automatically'). The flag does not exist — it's referenced as a future capability that's leaking into output.

Cardinality drifts are exactly the deterministic case for this:
- skill knows the file path, line number, and exact substitution
- substitution is mechanical (105 → 106, Prelude (4) → Prelude (5))
- no human judgment needed for the change itself

Implement --apply-trivial as a flag on /audit-project that:
- only applies [DOC FIX] items the skill marks as deterministic (cardinality count drifts; trivially-resolvable broken links to renamed-but-extant files; etc.)
- skips anything ambiguous (factual claims, behavior descriptions)
- prints what was applied for review
- leaves git in dirty state for user to commit/revert

title: Pick canonical room for audit-project results: <wing>/decisions vs <wing>/audit_results
description:
The /audit-project skill auto-saved its findings drawer to a NEW room tla_puzzles/audit_results during loom-b6o (2026-05-04). Loom self-audits historically file findings to loom/decisions. Today the convention drifts project-by-project — the skill creates whichever room it thinks fits.

Pick one convention and enforce it in the skill:
(a) audits go to <wing>/audit_results — separates audit findings from design decisions; pro: easier to filter/sweep audits later. Con: another room to remember; doesn't reuse the decisions room which is the natural home for 'this is what we found, here's what we should do'.
(b) audits go to <wing>/decisions — reuses existing convention; pro: one less room; audits ARE decisions about project state. Con: dilutes the decisions room with state snapshots.

Once decided, update the skill to hardcode the room choice and update existing drift (the new tla_puzzles/audit_results drawer may need to migrate or be left as historical).

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_598a609fc3e29b3c5937965f (loom/decisions, friction signal #2).
Trial: loom-b6o.
design:
DECISION (2026-05-04): option (b) — audit-derived drawers go to <wing>/decisions. Reuse the existing decisions room rather than create separate audit rooms.

Rationale:
- Decisions room already exists in every loom-managed project per convention. Zero new rooms.
- Audits ARE decisions about project state; every useful audit comes with recommendations, making them functionally 'we figured out X; here is what to do' content.
- Eliminates room proliferation (tla-puzzles trial created BOTH audit_results AND findings in one session — the same skill, no convention enforcement, two rooms).
- Easier discoverability — users know to look in decisions for 'what did we figure out about this project.'

Implementation:
- Update /audit-project skill to hardcode <wing>/decisions as the destination for all audit-derived drawers.
- Drawer title/content carries the 'this is an audit' semantic (e.g., 'Project audit: <project> (<date>)'). Room name does not encode the distinction.

Migration of existing trial drawers (3 in tla_puzzles wing): file as separate hygiene bead (P4 backlog). Existing drawers in audit_results / findings rooms remain as historical artifacts; the new convention applies to all future audits.
notes: 2026-05-04 update from same loom-b6o trial: the parallel session created a SECOND room in the same wing (tla_puzzles/findings) for the docs-generation meta-finding, in addition to tla_puzzles/audit_results created earlier in the same session. Same skill, same wing, two different rooms — the convention drift is worse than first reported. Whatever decision lands needs to enforce ONE room name across all audit-derived drawers (findings, results, gaps, etc. should not all become separate rooms).

title: audit-project: formalize or scope-out [DOC FIX][MIRROR] tag for root README
description:
The /audit-project skill emits a [DOC FIX][MIRROR] tag for drift in root README.md when README mirrors docs/index.md content, even though the skill's documented scope is docs/. Surfaced during loom-b6o (tla-puzzles trial, 2026-05-04) when 3 cardinality drifts in docs/index.md had identical mirror drifts in README.md and the skill proactively reported them.

Decision needed:
(a) Formalize MIRROR support — add a configurable list of files outside docs/ that mirror docs content (README.md is the canonical case), and document the tag in the skill's reference page.
(b) Scope strictly to docs/ — strip the MIRROR proactivity from the skill, document README-as-known-blind-spot, and let users run a separate root-readme-drift check.

Today the behavior is emergent and undocumented — different invocations may or may not produce MIRROR depending on what the model notices. Pick one and make it deterministic.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_598a609fc3e29b3c5937965f (loom/decisions, friction signal #1).
Trial: loom-b6o.
design:
DECISION (2026-05-04): option (c) reframe — drop the MIRROR tag concept entirely; expand /audit-project scope to 'all documentation files at the project root AND docs/' as one flat set. README.md is a sibling doc, not a mirror.

Default scope:
- README.md, README.rst, README.txt at project root
- docs/**/*.md (existing scope)

Out of default scope (excluded):
- AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md — agent-instructions, not user-docs (config-extendable later if needed)
- .github/*, package metadata files

Behavior change: drift in README reports as [DOC FIX] with no qualifier; the tag MIRROR is removed. Project-onboarder + audit-project skill reference docs need updating to reflect new scope.

Naturally handles loom-qp0/loom-3hb generated-docs case: when docs/ is gitignored and generated from README, audit reports drift in README (the source) without mirror-detection logic. The drift just appears there because README is in scope.

Known tradeoff (defer to a future bead, not blocking): if a project has README.md for users + docs/index.md for users with intentionally different content, audit will report 'drift' between them when there's no drift, just intentional divergence. Solve when encountered.

  5. Run /docs-scaffold UNLESS docs/ is generated. /docs-scaffold itself

title: km8.5 how-to: add migration playbook for projects with existing flat docs
description:
Trial outcome: HAW had 18 existing flat docs files (architecture.md, *-reference.md, spec.md, plans/, research/, etc.) before scaffold. M3's three options (skip/merge/refuse) handle file conflicts but say nothing about migration — the user is left with the new quadrant skeleton sitting alongside legacy flat content with no guidance on what should move where.

The km8.5 how-to (`docs/how-to/scaffold-managed-project-docs.md`) currently doesn't cover this path. Grep for 'migrat|existing|flat doc|move into' returns zero matches.

For projects already running loom long enough to accumulate docs/, this is the COMMON case — they don't get a clean scaffold; they get an overlay. The how-to needs a section that:

1. Shows how to classify existing flat docs into Diataxis bins (cheat-sheet: '`*-reference.md` → reference/, `architecture.md` / explanation-shaped → explanation/, '`*-guide.md` → how-to/, plans/research/ subdirs → keep as-is OR migrate to ADRs').
2. Names the 'half-migrated state' as expected and OK — projects can adopt quadrants for new content while leaving legacy files where they are.
3. Cross-references the audit-project drift checks: once the scaffold lands, future drift checks will fire on flat-vs-quadrant inconsistencies; the playbook should call this out.

Out of scope (v2 if it becomes a pain point): a /docs-migrate skill that auto-classifies. v1 is the human playbook in the how-to.

Source: loom-km8.6 HAW first-customer trial M3 phase, F6. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_9bebc79fba4116c3a8b2489a §F6 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-04).

title: /docs-scaffold: add --root flag for cross-project use (parity with /audit-project)
description:
audit-project supports `--root <path>` and `--wing <name>` (km8.4 portability). docs-scaffold M1 always resolves to cwd's git root with no override flag.

For cross-project trials (e.g. running scaffold against another project from inside loom), the operator has to `cd` to the target project first. Asymmetric ergonomics across the two skills, where the symmetric expectation would be that anything the audit can target, the scaffold can target too.

Fix: add `--root <path>` to docs-scaffold (M1 detection, M3 docs/.no-diataxis check, M4 git remote detection — all of which currently assume cwd — should resolve against the flagged root). Don't add --wing to docs-scaffold yet; substitution variables don't depend on it in v1 (the three vars are project_name / repo_url / short_description, all detected from the resolved root or prompted, none from MemPalace).

Source: loom-km8.6 HAW first-customer trial M1 (pre-flight observation; manifested in the workaround during M2-M6). Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_f2e3a5c273214cbf335a8918 §F5 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-04).

title: Disambiguate 'loom-managed' term between docs-scaffold and audit-project
description:
Two skills, two definitions of 'loom-managed':

- docs-scaffold M1: project is loom-managed iff `.claude/workflow.json` exists.
- audit-project Step 1 (loom-managed-for-docs-check heuristic): `.beads/` present AND `docs/` contains a Diataxis quadrant.

HAW satisfies the first but not the second. The audit-project heuristic correctly gates the docs-drift check (you can't drift-check what doesn't exist), but calling both 'loom-managed' overloads the term and made the M1 trial output confusing.

Fix: rename audit-project's heuristic — e.g. `has-diataxis-substrate`, `loom-managed-for-docs-drift`, or split the gate into two named conditions. Reserve 'loom-managed' for the workflow.json sense (docs-scaffold's definition) since that's the one matching how onboarding/audit speak about projects elsewhere.

Source: loom-km8.6 HAW first-customer trial M1 audit phase. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_f2e3a5c273214cbf335a8918 §F1 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-04).

close_reason: Renamed audit-project's docs-check gate from 'loom-managed' (heuristic) to 'has-diataxis-substrate' (.beads/ + Diataxis quadrant present). Reserved 'loom-managed' for the workflow.json sense matching docs-scaffold + project-onboarder. Added explicit disambiguation note where the term is introduced. project-onboarder is term-clean already (doesn't use the overloaded label).

title: install.sh: sweep orphan loom-owned symlinks before re-linking (Gap 2)
description:
## Symptom

When a loom skill / command / agent / hook / lib / script is renamed or deleted in the loom repo, the corresponding symlink under `~/.claude/{skills,agents,commands,hooks,lib,scripts}/` is left dangling. install.sh only ADDS new links; it never sweeps stale ones.

Today (2026-05-04, /wrap-up after loom-tag) the workaround lives in the /wrap-up command's deploy snippet:

```bash
LOOM_ROOT="$(pwd)"
for dir in skills agents commands hooks lib scripts; do
  find "$HOME/.claude/$dir" -maxdepth 3 -type l 2>/dev/null | while read -r link; do
    target=$(readlink "$link")
    case "$target" in
      "$LOOM_ROOT"/*)
        [ ! -e "$target" ] && rm "$link" && echo "  pruned dangling: $link"
        ;;
    esac
  done
done
```

This works but is duplicated outside install.sh. If a user runs `./install.sh` directly (skipping /wrap-up's prelude), dangling symlinks linger.

## Root cause

install.sh's `install_link` function only handles the "this source exists, link it" case. It never iterates the install destination to find symlinks whose loom-source no longer exists.

## Fix shape (option A from the original drawer)

Add a pre-link sweep step to install.sh:

```bash
# Before re-linking, prune dangling loom-owned symlinks.
log "Pruning dangling loom-owned symlinks..."
for dir in skills agents commands hooks lib scripts; do
  [ -d "$CLAUDE_HOME/$dir" ] || continue
  find "$CLAUDE_HOME/$dir" -maxdepth 3 -type l 2>/dev/null | while read -r link; do
    target=$(readlink "$link")
    case "$target" in
      "$LOOM_ROOT"/*)
        if [ ! -e "$target" ]; then
          do_or_print "rm '$link'"
          log "  pruned dangling: $link"
        fi
        ;;
    esac
  done
done
```

Place this **before** the per-primitive linking blocks (lines 93+). Preserve `.pre-loom.bak` files (they're regular files, not symlinks; the `-type l` filter already excludes them).

## Why-not alternatives (per original drawer)

- **Option B — separate `scripts/clean-stale-loom-links.sh`**: rejected. Looser coupling but adds a second step the user has to remember. Folding into install.sh keeps the contract "install.sh makes my ~/.claude/ match my loom checkout" idempotent.

## Cleanup follow-ups (folded into this fix)

Once Gap 2 ships, the /wrap-up command's deploy snippet's prune loop becomes redundant. Drop it as part of this bead — leave only `./install.sh` as the deploy command.

## Acceptance

- Renaming any one loom skill / command / agent / hook / lib / script and running `./install.sh` cleanly removes the now-dangling symlink without manual intervention
- `--check` (dry-run) mode reports which symlinks WOULD be pruned without removing them
- `.pre-loom.bak` files preserved (the `-type l` filter handles this naturally)
- /wrap-up command's deploy snippet simplified to just `./install.sh`
- Drawer `drawer_loom_decisions_5c6dbbce59f5373cf7b67935` updated to mark Gap 2 RESOLVED

## Out of scope

- Detecting symlinks that point at OTHER `$LOOM_ROOT`s (e.g., a user with two loom checkouts). install.sh is run from one specific loom checkout and only sweeps symlinks pointing at that checkout's tree. A user with multiple loom checkouts can run install.sh from each.

## Lineage

Sibling drawer with full lineage: `drawer_loom_decisions_5c6dbbce59f5373cf7b67935` ("install.sh — TWO RENAME-DEPLOY GAPS FOUND" → updated 2026-05-04 with Gap 3 + resolution status). This bead resolves the third remaining gap.
close_reason: Gap 2 resolved: install.sh prunes dangling loom-owned symlinks at start of link phase; smoke-tested with synthetic dangling symlink. /wrap-up snippet simplified to ./install.sh. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_5c6dbbce59f5373cf7b67935 marks all three gaps RESOLVED.

title: Add .claude/worktrees/ to .gitignore as part of loom setup process
description:
## Goal

Ensure that any loom-managed project gitignores `.claude/worktrees/` (the path where `Agent` tool's `isolation: "worktree"` parameter creates per-subagent worktrees). Today loom itself has untracked `.claude/worktrees/` clutter; same will hit any project once they start dispatching parallel workers.

## What to ship

Two pieces:

1. **Loom's own .gitignore** — append `.claude/worktrees/` to `/home/frank/repos/loom/.gitignore`. One-line patch. Loom is the canonical loom-managed project; eat-our-own-dogfood.

2. **Setup integration for managed projects** — pick the right surface:

   - `hooks/workflow-mode-onboarding.sh` — already fires once on first session in a workspace; could append `.claude/worktrees/` to the project's `.gitignore` if not present
   - `agents/project-onboarder.md` — could add a check (PASS if `.claude/worktrees/` is in `.gitignore`, INFO with one-line fix if not)
   - `commands/audit-project.md` (`--apply-trivial`) — bundle as a trivial doc-fix
   - `templates/diataxis/` — out of scope (templates/diataxis is doc-skeleton, not project bootstrap)

   Recommended: project-onboarder check (item 11, INFO-only with template fix). Mirrors the docs/.no-diataxis check shape (loom-km8.3 just shipped — INFO/PASS only, not load-bearing). The fix is "append `.claude/worktrees/` to .gitignore" — clean trivial-fix shape.

## Why this matters

Without the gitignore, `.claude/worktrees/` appears as untracked in every `git status` after any parallel-dispatch session. Worktrees are ephemeral (locked by parent claude pid; auto-clean on harness exit) — they should NEVER be tracked. Today's session left three locked-stale worktrees that linger across sessions until manually removed.

Lineage: `drawer_loom_decisions_df73c725b47dd67832935e3a` (loom-tag, the Agent isolation:worktree path-resolution finding) — same family of "parallel-dispatch hygiene" issues.

## Acceptance

- Loom's own `.gitignore` has `.claude/worktrees/` and `git status` no longer shows it as untracked
- New loom-managed projects get the entry automatically (via whichever setup surface lands the integration)
- For existing loom-managed projects, the surface (project-onboarder INFO check or `--apply-trivial`) suggests the fix at audit time

## Out of scope

- Cleaning up the existing locked-stale worktrees in loom (separate bead if needed; harness usually auto-cleans on exit)
- Auto-pruning worktrees programmatically (worktrees are managed by the harness, not by loom)
close_reason: loom-y7w: .gitignore patched + project-onboarder item 11 (INFO-only Diataxis-shape sibling). loom-5q9: drawer_loom_decisions_5c6dbbce59f5373cf7b67935 updated with Gap 3 + loom-1nf reference.

title: install.sh has hardcoded skill list; new skills don't deploy until install.sh is patched
description:
## Symptom

Today (2026-05-04) the loom-km8.2 bead added a new skill at
`skills/docs-scaffold/SKILL.md`. After running the standard /wrap-up
deploy sequence (mkdir loop + dangling-symlink prune + ./install.sh),
the new skill subdirectory existed at `~/.claude/skills/docs-scaffold/`
but was empty — install.sh did not create the SKILL.md symlink.

Manual workaround: `ln -sf $LOOM_ROOT/skills/docs-scaffold/SKILL.md
~/.claude/skills/docs-scaffold/SKILL.md`.

## Root cause

`install.sh` lines 93-101 invoke `install_link skills/<name>/SKILL.md`
**once per skill, with the skill name hardcoded**:

```bash
install_link skills/bugfix-a-bead/SKILL.md skills/bugfix-a-bead/SKILL.md
install_link skills/research-a-bead/SKILL.md skills/research-a-bead/SKILL.md
install_link skills/feature-a-bead/SKILL.md skills/feature-a-bead/SKILL.md
install_link skills/refactor-a-bead/SKILL.md skills/refactor-a-bead/SKILL.md
install_link skills/cleanup-a-bead/SKILL.md skills/cleanup-a-bead/SKILL.md
install_link skills/docs-a-bead/SKILL.md skills/docs-a-bead/SKILL.md
install_link skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md
install_link skills/session-startup/SKILL.md skills/session-startup/SKILL.md
```

Adding a new skill requires patching install.sh in lockstep. This
is similar in shape to install.sh gap 1 (mkdir for new subdirs)
documented in the "install.sh — TWO RENAME-DEPLOY GAPS FOUND"
drawer (loom/decisions, 2026-05-03), but is a third distinct gap
that drawer didn't capture.

## Fix shape

Replace the explicit list with a glob loop:

```bash
log "Linking skills..."
for skill_md in skills/*/SKILL.md; do
  install_link "$skill_md" "$skill_md"
done
```

Symmetric for commands, agents, hooks, lib, scripts (some may
already use globs — check). The `for d in skills/*/; do mkdir -p
"$HOME/.claude/$d"; done` workaround in /wrap-up's deploy snippet
already creates parent dirs from a glob, so the install.sh side
just needs to follow the same pattern.

## Acceptance

- Adding a new skill to `skills/<new-name>/SKILL.md` and running
  `./install.sh` symlinks it without further changes
- All existing skills still symlink (regression-safe)
- The /wrap-up deploy snippet's mkdir loop becomes redundant once
  install.sh creates parent dirs itself (file as a follow-up
  cleanup bead, or fold into this fix)
- Update the "install.sh — TWO RENAME-DEPLOY GAPS FOUND" drawer
  with a note referencing this bead's resolution

## Out of scope

- Permanent removal of the /wrap-up deploy prelude (mkdir + prune)
  — that's a follow-up cleanup bead once both gaps are fixed.
close_reason: install.sh:93-97 globs skills/*/SKILL.md; verified all 10 skills (incl. docs-scaffold + audit-project) symlinked. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_5c6dbbce59f5373cf7b67935 updated with resolution status; Gap 1 was already silently fixed; Gap 2 still open (manual prune in /wrap-up).

title: loom-a3l layer 3: file upstream issue against MemPalace plugin for opaque -32000 error on param-name mismatch
description:
Layer 3 of the three-layer fix from drawer drawer_loom_decisions_0065525490e0e4780f73670a ("MEMPALACE_DIARY_WRITE — PARAM IS \`entry\`, NOT \`content\`", 2026-05-04).

Layers 1 (loom-a3l, closed) and 2 (separate bead, layer 2) are workarounds. Layer 3 is the real fix: the MemPalace plugin returns \`MCP error -32000: Internal tool error\` when an MCP tool is called with a missing-required or wrong-named parameter, with no further detail naming the offending parameter.

The expected behavior is a structured error message identifying which parameter was missing or misnamed (e.g. \`Missing required parameter \`entry\` for tool mempalace_diary_write\`). Most MCP servers do this; the MemPalace plugin doesn't.

Reproduction:
1. Call \`mempalace_diary_write\` with \`agent_name='X'\` and \`content='Y'\` (omitting the required \`entry\` param and substituting an incorrect one).
2. Observe \`-32000 Internal tool error\` with no further information.
3. Confirm via ToolSearch that the schema requires \`entry\`, not \`content\`.

Acceptance:
- Issue filed in the MemPalace plugin's upstream tracker (find via the plugin source — likely a GitHub repo associated with the mempalace MCP server).
- Issue body includes: the reproduction steps above, the expected vs actual behavior, the MCP error code, and a pointer to drawer drawer_loom_decisions_0065525490e0e4780f73670a as the lineage trace.
- bd close references the upstream issue URL in --reason.

Investigation hint:
- mempalace plugin path: ~/.claude/plugins/ or wherever Claude Code installs plugins.
- The plugin manifest likely points at a git repo; that's the issue tracker.

Source-of-truth drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_0065525490e0e4780f73670a.
close_reason: filed upstream issue: https://github.com/MemPalace/mempalace/issues/1351 — opaque -32000 on param-name mismatch, with reproduction + expected behavior + impact section.

title: loom: spell out mempalace_diary_write param=entry in skills/commands/how-tos
description:
The mempalace_diary_write MCP tool requires `entry` (not `content`) as its main parameter. The plugin returns `-32000 Internal tool error` with no further detail when called with the wrong param name, so the failure mode is discoverable only by reading the schema via ToolSearch. During /wrap-up after closing loom-km8.1, this caused three failed diary writes before the schema was checked.

Fix: patch every loom skill / command / how-to that instructs an agent to call mempalace_diary_write to spell out the parameter name inline. Keep the patch lightweight (`(param: \`entry\`)` after the tool name) so future agents see the param without needing to fetch the schema.

Files in scope (operative call sites first):
- commands/wrap-up.md (step 3, mempalace section)
- skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md (D3, step 3)
- docs/how-to/stop-a-session.md (step 3)
- docs/how-to/finish-a-bead.md (step 8)
- docs/reference/mempalace-mcp.md (catalogue line)
- docs/how-to/where-to-update-what.md (table)
- docs/reference/decision-tables.md (table)
- docs/tutorials/bug-walkthrough.md (verify shows correct params)
- docs/tutorials/feature-walkthrough.md (verify shows correct params)

Acceptance:
- Every operative loom file naming mempalace_diary_write makes `entry` visible at the call site.
- Tutorials show a correct example invocation (already do; verify).
- No behavior change in primitives — pure docs/skill text.

Source-of-truth drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_0065525490e0e4780f73670a ("MEMPALACE_DIARY_WRITE — PARAM IS `entry`, NOT `content`", 2026-05-04).

title: T6: HAW first-customer trial (research)
description:
Goal: Run /docs-scaffold against HAW as the first-customer trial; capture friction systematically; file follow-up beads for issues found. NO commit to HAW unless user approves the outcome separately. Depends on T2/T3/T4 closing.

Per D1 §OPEN: shape defaulted to research (drawer-as-deliverable for friction audit). User can switch to docs-shape (just-make-the-docs-exist) at claim time.

Source-of-truth: loom-9z1.10 D1 plan §E (drawer_loom_decisions_63aadc6e849779a509678d90).

Steps:
1. cd into HAW worktree.
2. Run /audit-project — confirm HAW is loom-managed (workflow.json present); confirm new T3 check fires INFO suggesting /docs-scaffold.
3. Run /docs-scaffold — capture friction at each M1-M6 step (refusals, surprises, file-diff approval moments).
4. Review the proposed diff — note what HAW would want different (label renames? Different primitive globs? Other?).
5. DO NOT commit unless user approves. Capture decision-drawer + KG triples in HAW's wing if user opts to keep the scaffold.
6. File follow-up beads for any friction needing fix in loom (T1-T4 follow-ups).

Cross-wing tunnel candidate (per D1 §lineage): draw a tunnel from D1's drawer (loom/decisions) to HAW's eventual /docs-scaffold trial drawer.

Acceptance:
- Friction audit drawer landed in loom/decisions (or HAW's wing if user kept the scaffold) summarizing what worked + what surprised
- 0+ follow-up beads filed in loom for any drift surfaced
- User decision recorded: keep HAW scaffold or revert

title: T3: project-onboarder 10th check (Diataxis-shape, INFO-only)

title: T2: /docs-scaffold skill + command
description:

title: Session-close 'git pull --rebase' linearizes --no-ff merge commits
description:
The session-close protocol in loom CLAUDE.md says `git pull --rebase` before `git push`. When the local main branch contains `--no-ff` merge commits (the convention used to integrate bead branches), rebase flattens those merge bubbles into linear work commits, losing the integration-boundary information.

**Evidence (2026-05-04, this session):**

- Merged `frank/loom-9z1.8` to main with `--no-ff` → merge commit 9950b37.
- Merged `frank/loom-9z1.9` to main with `--no-ff` → merge commit 6ee0e62.
- Final `git pull --rebase` before push linearized both: the work commits c5fa4a2 (9z1.9) became 6f3e0cb, and the explicit merge-bubble commits 9950b37 / 6ee0e62 vanished from history.
- Push succeeded but origin/main no longer shows the integration boundaries that the worker session intentionally created via `--no-ff`.

**Impact:**

- 'Where was bead X integrated?' no longer has a clean answer via git log shape — must `git log --grep` instead.
- The `--no-ff` convention itself is undermined: doing it during merge then losing it at push wastes the discipline.
- Cosmetic for single-bead sessions, but worse for parallel-dispatch sessions like today's where the merge bubbles distinguished concurrent work.

**Root cause:** Convention conflict.
- `--no-ff` during merge: 'preserve integration boundary as a merge commit.'
- `git pull --rebase` at session close: 'keep main linear, avoid spurious merge commits when origin has moved.'
- The two conventions assume different priorities about history shape; combining them naively destroys the first.

**Proposed fix shapes (to triage during work):**

- **A. Drop `--no-ff` on merges; accept linear history.** Lose: integration boundaries documented in log shape. Keep: simpler model. Doesn't match the existing convention or the 'one bead = one branch' framing where the merge commit IS the integration record.
- **B. Replace `git pull --rebase` with `git pull --no-rebase` (or plain `git pull`).** Lose: linear-by-default at session close; gain: merge commits preserved. May create spurious merge commits when origin has moved with no local conflicts.
- **C. Use `git pull --rebase=merges` at session close.** Rebases linear commits onto origin while preserving merge commits as merges. This is exactly the case the flag was designed for. Likely the right knob.
- **D. Configure `pull.rebase = merges` globally on the repo (`git config pull.rebase merges`) so plain `git pull` does the right thing.** Same effect as C but doesn't require remembering the flag in the CLAUDE.md protocol. Cleanest.
- **E. Skip session-close pull entirely; rely on `git push` with explicit upstream-divergence handling.** Surgical but risky if origin diverges.

**Default recommendation pending brainstorm:** D — set repo-local `pull.rebase = merges` so the existing CLAUDE.md text (`git pull --rebase`) does the right thing without changes to the protocol prose. Belt-and-suspenders: also update CLAUDE.md to mention the config + spell out `--rebase=merges` for clarity.

**Acceptance:**

- Run a parallel-dispatch session like today's (multiple `--no-ff` merges to main, then session-close push). Verify origin/main retains the merge-bubble commits.
- CLAUDE.md session-close section reflects whatever the chosen mechanism is — either updated command or explanatory note about the config.

**Out of scope:** changing the existing `--no-ff` merge convention; only the pull-side interaction. Existing local-history of past sessions stays linearized.

**Recipe shape:** bugfix-a-bead. No worktree (CLAUDE.md edit + maybe one git-config command).

title: /audit-project --check=docs: drift detection vs system + beads + MemPalace
description:
Goal: Extend /audit-project with a docs alignment check. Per the precedence rule (when docs disagree with system / beads / MemPalace, docs lose), the check reports doc fixes — never the other way around.

Files: skills/audit-project/SKILL.md (add --check=docs flag + checks), agents/project-onboarder.md (extend scan logic if needed).

Checks (v1, naive grep):
1. **Cardinality** — docs that count things ('All six commands have disable-model-invocation'); grep source-of-truth, compare. Catches the loom-469 class.
2. **Citation resolution** — every bead-ID pattern (loom-[a-z0-9]{3}), commit SHA, file path, drawer slug, slash command name in docs/ must resolve. `bd show` for beads, `git cat-file -e` for commits, filesystem checks for paths, mempalace_search for drawer slugs.
3. **Behavior claims** — docs that say 'X exists' / 'X does Y' against system reality. E.g., docs claim /feature-a-bead exists → check commands/feature-a-bead.md. Catches the loom-qj3 lying-doc bug class at audit time.
4. **Inclusion glob coverage** — every primitive on disk → has a doc page (auto-discovered); every doc page → corresponds to a real primitive. Symmetric check.
5. **Explanation-doc consistency** — explanation docs cite drawers; verify each via mempalace_get_drawer (or near-match via mempalace_search if slug is partial title).

Output: structured report — one line per drift item, tagged [DOC FIX], with what doc says, what reality says, suggested fix.

Flags:
- --apply-trivial — auto-apply count corrections + dead-bead-ID replacement (per supersedes-chain).
- Default behavior for loom-managed projects: docs check runs by default when /audit-project is invoked.

Out of scope (v2 if needed): semantic LLM-based claim extraction.

Acceptance:
- Running /audit-project --check=docs against current loom catches the loom-qj3 / loom-469 class of issues.
- No false positives for loom's healthy state (verified by running on a known-good commit).
- --apply-trivial cleanly fixes count / dead-bead drift without touching anything else.

Recipe shape: feature-a-bead. Depends on A1 + A2 (the Reference quadrant must exist for inclusion-glob check to have a target).

- README + CLAUDE.md current.

title: bd pre-commit hook exports issues.jsonl to worktree root instead of .beads/
description:
The bd pre-commit hook (installed via .beads/hooks/pre-commit, which calls 'bd hooks run pre-commit') exports issues.jsonl to the WORKTREE ROOT when commit happens inside a git worktree (e.g. .worktrees/loom-qj3/), instead of (or in addition to) the canonical .beads/issues.jsonl path.

**Symptom**: during the loom-qj3 commit (e7d853c), the export ran with this output: 'Exported 14 issues to /home/frank/repos/loom/.worktrees/loom-qj3/.beads/issues.jsonl' — but the resulting commit included a NEW tracked file at the worktree root: 'create mode 100644 issues.jsonl' (14 lines). The .beads/issues.jsonl path was also touched. So either bd exported to BOTH places, or the export message lied about the path.

**Reproduction**: from within a git worktree under .worktrees/<name>/, run a commit. Observe that 'git show HEAD --stat' includes a stray 'issues.jsonl' at root not just at .beads/issues.jsonl.

**Self-defeating cleanup**: attempting 'git rm issues.jsonl && git commit' to clean up produces an empty commit (b20ac95 in loom-qj3 worktree) — the pre-commit hook re-exports the file during the cleanup commit, re-staging it with the same content as HEAD, so the deletion gets reverted before the commit is captured. Net: the stray tracked-but-missing-on-disk file persists in HEAD across cleanup attempts.

**Impact**: cosmetic but annoying. The stray file shows as 'deleted' in 'git status' indefinitely (since HEAD has it but disk doesn't), creating perpetual visual noise. Doesn't affect bd dolt push or sync since those use the canonical .beads/issues.jsonl path.

**Investigation pointers**:
- /home/frank/repos/loom/.beads/hooks/pre-commit (the bd-installed hook stub that calls 'bd hooks run pre-commit')
- bd CLI's worktree-detection logic (does 'bd hooks run pre-commit' detect git_dir vs worktree path correctly?)
- The 'Warning: .../.beads has permissions 0775 (recommended: 0700)' message that fires from worktrees but not from main repo — adjacent symptom suggesting bd's worktree handling may have multiple sharp edges.

**Proposed fix shape**: bd's pre-commit export logic should resolve the canonical .beads/issues.jsonl path via 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' or equivalent, and write only there. If a stray issues.jsonl exists at any other path, leave it alone (don't re-export there).

**Workaround until fixed**: do the commit from the main repo path (cd /home/frank/repos/loom && git commit) instead of from the worktree, OR accept the stray file and clean it up via a manual cleanup commit AFTER the main bead has been merged + the worktree removed.

Out of scope: changing the canonical export path; only the worktree-context bug needs fixing.
close_reason: Defensive workaround shipped: /issues.jsonl in .gitignore (root-anchored, doesn't affect canonical .beads/issues.jsonl) + CLAUDE.md 'Don't' note recommending main-path commits over worktree-mode commits. Upstream fix in beads CLI's worktree-detection is a separate concern; loom only defends against the bug's footprint. commit a1bda4e.

title: git-push-bd-sync hook false-positive: tighten matcher when push target ≠ cwd .beads/
description: When pushing repo A from a cwd inside repo B with dirty .beads/, the git-push-bd-sync hook warns about B's beads even though the push is for A. Surfaced 2026-05-03 during loom extraction (multiple loom pushes triggered the warning because cwd was HAW with dirty .beads/). Fix: detect whether the dirty .beads/ belongs to the same repo as the push target. Only warn if the same. Use git rev-parse --show-toplevel + comparison with the remote URL git is pushing to. Document the new behavior in docs/manual.md hooks reference.
close_reason: Hook now resolves push target via cd-chain parsing; .beads/ presence and dirty-check run against target dir, not cwd. 11 fixture tests under lib/tests/. Docs updated. Light-mode close.

title: bd pre-commit hook: defer auto-export when user is staging non-bd files
description:
Follow-up to loom-cka. The bd pre-commit hook auto-exports '.beads/issues.jsonl' on every commit, including the first commit after 'bd hooks install' when the user is mid-way through staging unrelated files. Result: two-things-in-one-commit against intent.

loom-cka mitigates the symptom on the loom side via doc/onboarder guidance to run an absorbing bd-only commit before any logical commit. The root fix lives in bd itself: detect 'is the user actively staging non-bd files?' and defer the auto-export to a separate commit when so.

This is a bd-repo change, NOT loom — file the upstream issue against the beads repo. Tracked here so the loom-side mitigation has an upstream pointer.

Source drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_dfb5ece53785610c83ca6619 (loom-b6o close-out, friction signal E section, option (c)). Lineage: loom-cka, loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial 2026-05-04, observed 891d2ae cardinality commit silently picked up +6-line .beads/issues.jsonl re-export.

title: Update 'install.sh — TWO RENAME-DEPLOY GAPS FOUND' drawer to reference loom-1nf as the third gap
description:
## Goal

The existing decision drawer "install.sh — TWO RENAME-DEPLOY GAPS FOUND" (loom/decisions wing, ~2026-05-03) documents two gaps in install.sh:

- Gap 1 — needs `mkdir -p` for new skill subdirs
- Gap 2 — dangling symlinks from renamed/removed primitives

A third gap was discovered today during loom-km8 wrap-up: `install.sh` lines 93-101 have a hardcoded list of `install_link skills/<name>/SKILL.md` calls instead of globbing `skills/*/SKILL.md`. New skills don't deploy without patching the list. Filed as **loom-1nf**.

## What to ship

Update the existing drawer to add a "Gap 3" section referencing loom-1nf, OR — if loom-1nf ships first — fold this update into loom-1nf's closing drawer (which would naturally cite + extend the original gaps drawer).

Locate the drawer slug via `mempalace_search "install.sh rename-deploy gaps"` filtered by wing=loom.

## Acceptance

- Either: drawer updated with Gap 3 + loom-1nf reference
- Or: loom-1nf's closing drawer cites the original drawer and adds Gap 3 to the lineage, AND a tunnel is drawn between the two drawers

## Why low priority (P4)

Decision drawers age gracefully. The original drawer is still correct on its two gaps; the third is captured in loom-1nf's bead description and (eventually) loom-1nf's closing drawer. Adding the cross-reference is hygiene, not load-bearing. Bump to P3 if loom-1nf's closing drawer doesn't naturally cite the original.
